title: Window of Tolerance Tracker
description: Track your daily nervous system state to build awareness of your window of tolerance — the zone where you can think clearly, feel emotions without being overwhelmed, and function effectively.
instructions: Complete this check-in once daily, ideally at the same time each day. There are no right or wrong answers — this is about building self-awareness of your nervous system patterns.

fields.label: Overall nervous system state right now

options.label: Hyperarousal — anxious, on edge, racing thoughts, irritable, can't relax
Window of Tolerance — calm enough to think, present, able to function
Hypoarousal — numb, disconnected, shut down, foggy, can't feel much

fields.label: Arousal level (1 = completely shut down, 5 = calm and present, 10 = extremely activated/overwhelmed)
What body signals do you notice?
fields.placeholder: e.g., tight shoulders, racing heart, heavy limbs, relaxed muscles, stomach tension, numb hands

fields.label: Did anything push you outside your window today?
fields.placeholder: e.g., loud noise startled me, argument at work, news report, nightmare last night, nothing specific

fields.label: What coping or grounding strategies did you use today?
fields.placeholder: e.g., 5-4-3-2-1 grounding, slow breathing, went for a walk, called a friend, none today

fields.label: How was your sleep last night? (1 = terrible, 10 = excellent)
Anything else you want to note about today?
fields.placeholder: e.g., good day overall, felt more present than usual, anniversary coming up so expecting harder days
